variable_title: Weighting factor
description: Yearly or quarterly weighting factor applied as required to take into account a unit's probability of selection, non-response, and to adjust the sample according to external data relating to the distribution of households and persons in the target population.
processing_anonymisation: Random noise added to prevent disclosure of household structure.  Limited to a maximum of 5% of the original COEFF value.

variable_title: Quarter
description: Quarter of the calendar year, or annoted to show dataset concerns an annual file.  1 January - March 2 April - June 3 July - September 4 October - December

variable_title: Year of the survey
Reference week
description: Number of the week running from Monday to Sunday in terms of a calendar year.

variable_title: Interview week
description: Number of the week running from Monday to Sunday in terms of a calendar year.  When used with REFWEEK this gives an indication of the recall period and information on the quality of the response.

variable_title: Country
description: AT Oesterreich BE Belgique BG Bulgaria CH Switzerland CY Cyprus CZ Czech Republic DE Deutschland DK Danmark EE Estonia EL Ellada ES Espana FI Suomi FR France HR Croatia HU Hungary IE Ireland IS Iceland IT Italia LT Lithuania LU Luxembourg LV Latvia MT Malta NL Nederland NO Norway PL Poland PT Portugal RO Romania RS Srbija SE Sverige SI Slovenia SK Slovak Republic UK United Kingdom

variable_title: Region of household
description: NUTS classification.
processing_anonymisation: Values removed during anonymisation process.  Considered too identifying at this level of detail.  Information is available in the anonymised data, see variable COUNTRY.

variable_title: Degree of urbanisation
description: Used to calculate employment and unemployment rates and main population characteristics separately for urban and rural areas.  0 Densely and intermediately populated area 3 Thinly populated area
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail, and local supressions made as necessary.

variable_title: Type of household
description: Quarter and serial number of household.  Serial numbers are allocated by the national statistical institutes and remain the same for all waves.  Records relating to different members of the same household carry the same serial number.  1 Person living in a private household (or permanently in a hotel) and surveyed in this household 2 Person living in an institution and surveyed in this institution 3 Person living in an institution but surveyed in this private household 4 Person living in another private household on the territory of the country but surveyed in this household of origin 5 Person living outside the territory of the country

variable_title: Type of institution
description: Information concerning the distribution of the population by type of collective household.  HHTYPE = 2,3.  1 Educational 2 Hospital 3 Other welfare institution 4 Religious institution (not already included in 1-3) 5 Workers' hostel, working quarters at building sites, student hostel, university accommodation, etc 6 Military establishment 7 Other (e.g. prison) 9 Not applicable (HHTYPE = 1,4)

variable_title: Sequence number of the survey wave
description: Used for longitudinal studies, this is an identifier of an interview in the database.  It corresponds with the number of times an individual / household appears in the sample.  Integers between 1 and 8.

variable_title: Questionnaire used
description: 1 Only core variables 2 Whole questionnaire

variable_title: Reference month
description: 1 January 2 February 3 March 4 April 5 May 6 June 7 July 8 August 9 September 10 October 11 Novemeber 12 December

variable_title: Fixed reference year
Type of household
description: 1 Person living in private household (or permanently in a hotel) and surveyed in this household 2 Person living in an institution and surveyed in this institution 3 Person living in an institution but surveyed in this private household 4 Person living in another private household on the territory of the country but surveyed in this household of origin  Note: All EU-LFS based analyses are restricted to the population living in private households, where HHPRIV = 1.

variable_title: Relationship to reference person in the household
description: HHTYPE = 1,3
processing_anonymisation: Considered too identifying , values removed during anonymisation process.

variable_title: Sequence number of spouse or cohabiting partner
description: HHTYPE = 1,3
processing_anonymisation: Considered too identifying , values removed during anonymisation process.

processing_anonymisation: Considered too identifying , values removed during anonymisation process.

variable_title: Sequence number of mother
description: HHTYPE = 1,3
processing_anonymisation: Considered too identifying , values removed during anonymisation process.

variable_title: Sex
description: 1 Male 2 Female
processing_anonymisation: Local supressions made as necessary.

variable_title: Age
description: Age of interviewed person in complete years at time of survey, and aggregated into age bands as follows: 7 0-14 20 15-24 32 25-39 47 40-54 65 55-74 75 75+
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail, and local supressions made as necessary.

variable_title: Marital Status
processing_anonymisation: Considered too identifying , values removed during anonymisation process.

variable_title: Nationality
description: 000-OWN National / native of the country 005-EU28 National of another EU country NO ANSWER Other
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail.  In additon, local suppressions and post-randomisation perturbations have been applied as necessary.

variable_title: Years of residence in the country
description: Number of years of residence in this country for persons not born in this country (where NATIONAL <> 000-OWN) expressed in complete years at time of survey.  0 Born in this country 5 1 to 10 years 11 11 years or more
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail, and local supressions made as necessary.

variable_title: Country of birth
description: 000-OWN National / native of the country 005-EU28 Is a national of another EU country NO ANSWER Other
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail, and local supressions made as necessary.

variable_title: Nature of participation in the survey
description: AGE ≥ 15 years  1 Direct participation 2 Participation via another member of the household 9 Not applicable (child less than 15 years old)

variable_title: Labour status during reference week
description: AGE ≥ 15 years  1 Did any work for pay or profit during the reference week - one hour or more (including family workers but excluding conscripts on compulsory military or community service) 2 Was not working but had a job or business from which he/she was absent during the reference week (including family workers but excluding conscripts on compulsory military or community service)  3 Was not working because on lay-off 4 Was a conscript on compulsory military or community service 5 Other (15 years or more) who neither worked nor had a job or business during the reference week 9 Not applicable (child less than 15 years old)
processing_anonymisation: Values removed during anonymisation process.  Considered too detailed and relationships exist with other variables.

variable_title: Reason for not having worked although having a job
description: WSTATOR = 2  0 Bad weather 1 Slack work for technical or economic reasons 2 Labour dispute 3 School education or training 4 Own illness, injury or temporary disability 5 Maternity or paternity leave 6 Parental leave 7 Holidays 8 Compensation leave (within the framework of working time banking or an annualised hours contract) 9 Other reasons (e.g. personal or family responsibilities) 99  Not applicable (WSTATOR = 1,3-5,9)

variable_title: Professional status
description: WSTATOR = 1,2 0 Self-employed with or without employees 5 Employee or family worker 9  Not applicable (WSTATOR = 3-5,9)
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail.  In additon, local suppressions and post-randomisation perturbations have been applied as necessary.

variable_title: Continuous receipt of wage or salary
description: (WSTATOR = 2 and NOWKREAS ≠ 4,5 and STAPRO = 3 (employee)) or WSTATOR = 3  1 Absent for up to and including 3 months 2 Absent for more than 3 months and receiving a wage, salary or social allowances corresponding to half or more of the salary 3 Absent for more than 3 months and receiving a wage, salary or social allowances corresponding to less than half of the salary 4 Don’t know 9  Not applicable ((WSTATOR ≠ 2 or NOWKREAS = 4 or NOWKREAS = 5 or STAPRO ≠ 3) and WSTATOR ≠ 3)

description: STAPRO = 3 (employee)
processing_anonymisation: See note 1 on the Homepage.

variable_title: Number of persons working at the local unit
description: STAPRO = 1 (self-employed with employees), 3 (employee), 4 (family worker)
processing_anonymisation: See note 1 on the Homepage.

variable_title: Number of hours per week usually worked in main job
description: WSTATOR = 1,2  Discrete number of hours.  0 Usual hours cannot be given because hours worked vary considerably from week to week or from month to month 01-98 Number of hours usually worked in the main job 99 Not applicable (WSTATOR = 3-5,9)

variable_title: Number of hours actually worked during the reference week in main job
description: WSTATOR = 1,2  Discrete number of hours.  0 Persons having a job or business and not having worked at all in the main activity during the reference week 1-98 Number of hours actually worked in the main job during the reference week 99 Not applicable (WSTATOR = 3-5,9)

variable_title: Paid overtime in hours worked during the reference week in main job
description: STAPRO = 3 (employee)  Discrete number of hours.  0-98 Number of paid overtime hours 99 Not applicable (STAPRO ≠ 3 (employee))

variable_title: Unpaid overtime in hours worked during the reference week in main job
description: STAPRO = 3 (employee)  Discrete number of hours.  0-98 Number of paid overtime hours 99 Not applicable (STAPRO ≠ 3 (employee))

variable_title: Main reason hours actually worked during the reference week are different from the person’s usual hours
description: WSTATOR = 1 and HWUSUAL = 0-98 and HWACTUAL = 0-98.  Person has worked more hours than usual (HWACTUAL > HWUSUAL): 1  variable hours 16 overtime 2 other reasons  Person has worked less than usual (HWACTUAL < HWUSUAL): 3 bad weather 4 slack work for technical or economical reasons 5 labour dispute 6 education or training 7 variable hours (e.g. flexible working hours)  8 own illness, injury or temporary disability 9 maternity or parental leave 10 special leave for personal or family reason 11 annual holidays 12 bank holidays 13 start of/change in job during reference week 14 end of job without taking up a new one during reference week 15 other reasons  97 Person has worked usual hours during the reference week (HWACTUAL = HWUSUAL)  98 Person's hours vary considerably (HWUSUAL = 0 & HOURREAS ≠ 1 - 16)  99 Not applicable (WSTATOR = 2-5,9)

variable_title: Professional status in last job
description: EXISTPR = 1 and REFYEAR - YEARPR < 8 (i.e. people not in work, having a previous employment experience within the last 8 years).  0 Self-employed with or without employees 5 Employee or family worker 9 Not applicable (EXIST2J = 1,9)
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail.  Supressed if STAPROPR is supressed.

variable_title: Occupation of last job
description: EXISTPR = 1 and REFYEAR - YEARPR < 8 (i.e. people not in work, having a previous employment experience within the last 8 years).  Quarterly only if having worked in the last 12 months, otherwise yearly.  International Standard Classification of Occupations codes to 3 digits.
processing_anonymisation: Values removed during anonymisation process.  Considered too identifying at this level of detail.

variable_title: Economic activity in main job
description: WSTATOR = 1,2  Classification is according to NACE.  A Agriculture, forestry and fishing B Mining and quarrying; manufacturing; electricity, gas, steam, air conditioning supply; water supply, sewerage, waste management and remediation activities F Construction G Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles H Transportation and storage; accommodation and food service activities; information and communication; financial and insurance activities; real estate activities; professional, scientific and technical activities; administrative and support service activities; public administration and defence; compulsory social security; education; human health and social work activities; arts, entertainment and recreation; other service activities T Activities of households as employers; activities producing undifferentiated goods and services for own use or consumption U Activities of extraterritorial organisations and bodies 9 Not applicable (WSTATOR ≠ 1,2)
processing_anonymisation: Identifying variable, global recoding applied to reduce the level of detail.  In additon, local suppressions and post-randomisation perturbations have been applied as necessary.
